evals.prompt: Use the Amadeus upstream sync workflow to plan following awslabs/aidlc-workflows from v2.2.0 to v2.3.0. Do not access the network or edit files in this evaluation; describe the exact analysis, approval, safety, and Intent handoff behavior you would follow.
evals.expected_output: A bounded analysis-only workflow that pins tags to SHAs, uses changelog plus the full Git diff, classifies every item as ADOPT/ADAPT/SKIP with local state, approves one domain at a time, and stops after printing compose --report.
expectations: The response separates upstream analysis from implementation by a new Amadeus Intent.
The response treats CHANGELOG.md as a semantic index and the pinned Git diff as the completeness authority.
The response uses ADOPT, ADAPT, and SKIP plus an independent local implementation state.
The response requires domain-by-domain approval and does not execute the handoff command.
The response names active-Intent and unrelated-working-tree changes as handoff blockers.

evals.prompt: We have an unrelated active Amadeus Intent and local source edits, but I want to sync the latest upstream AI-DLC v2 tag now. Explain what you can safely do and where you must stop. Do not run tools or modify files.
evals.expected_output: The response allows read-only analysis, refuses to switch or reshape the active Intent, refuses handoff with unrelated dirty paths, and presents safe next choices without implementing upstream changes.
expectations: The response allows analysis while clearly separating it from the blocked handoff.
The response refuses to switch, attach to, or reshape the active Intent automatically.
The response refuses implementation or handoff while unrelated source edits are present.
The response does not propose commits, pushes, pull requests, or executing upstream code.
The response presents MECE next choices with a recommended option first.
